content.Text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 13:31 CDT] You are doing a complete audit and fix of three GA4 dashboard HTML files. These files have had multiple partial edits and have bugs. Your job is to read each file carefully, understand the full DATA object and render logic, and make sure everything works correctly for ALL button combinations. ## THE REQUIREMENT Each dashboard has: - 3 range buttons: Last 30 Days, Last 60 Days, Last 90 Days - 3 compare buttons: vs Prior Year, vs Previous Period, No Compare Every combination of range + compare must show real data in the summary cards. No `—` dashes unless "No Compare" is selected. ## FILES 1. `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/ga4-dashboards/brian-harris-bmw-dashboard.html` 2. `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/ga4-dashboards/audi-baton-rouge-dashboard.html` 3. `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/ga4-dashboards/bmw-jackson-dashboard.html` ## REQUIRED DATA STRUCTURE Each file's `const DATA = {}` object must contain ALL of these keys with real sessions/users/newUsers/pageviews values: | Key | What it represents | |---|---| | `last30` | Last 30 days | | `last60` | Last 60 days | | `q1_2026` | Last 90 days | | `prev30` | Previous 30 days (days 31-60 ago) | | `prev60` | Previous 60 days (days 61-120 ago) | | `prev90` | Previous 90 days (days 91-180 ago) | | `yoy30` | Same 30 days last year | | `yoy60` | Same 60 days last year | | `yoy90` | Same 90 days last year | Each entry needs: `label`, `sessions`, `users`, `newUsers`, `pageviews`, `pagesPerSession`, `daily: {labels: [...], sessions: [...]}` **IMPORTANT:** The `daily.labels` and `daily.sessions` arrays must have the SAME LENGTH across all datasets used in a dashboard. If they don't match, Chart.js crashes and cards don't render. Use 11 items in all daily arrays. ## REAL DATA TO USE ### Brian Harris BMW - last30: sessions:21951, users:17730, newUsers:12028, pageviews:82634 - prev30: sessions:24332, users:20013, newUsers:14318, pageviews:85907 - yoy30: sessions:19800, users:16365, newUsers:11110, pageviews:72821 - last60: sessions:46283, users:37743, newUsers:26346, pageviews:168541 - prev60: sessions:43139, users:36026, newUsers:25206, pageviews:152293 - yoy60: sessions:41691, users:34406, newUsers:23869, pageviews:149966 - q1_2026: sessions:65885, users:54135, newUsers:37862, pageviews:243571 - prev90: sessions:68762, users:56907, newUsers:39434, pageviews:234292 - yoy90: sessions:61092, users:50642, newUsers:35358, pageviews:221519 ### Audi Baton Rouge - last30: sessions:16279, users:14030, newUsers:11311, pageviews:38059 - prev30: sessions:13993, users:11796, newUsers:9522, pageviews:39173 - yoy30: sessions:18293, users:15633, newUsers:12462, pageviews:40328 - last60: sessions:30272, users:25826, newUsers:20833, pageviews:77232 - prev60: sessions:24491, users:20789, newUsers:16421, pageviews:57293 - yoy60: sessions:36935, users:31512, newUsers:25274, pageviews:84220 - q1_2026: sessions:42974, users:36530, newUsers:29194, pageviews:109189 - prev90: sessions:25506, users:20945, newUsers:15450, pageviews:58408 - yoy90: sessions:55398, users:47744, newUsers:38920, pageviews:124617 ### BMW of Jackson - last30: sessions:26769, users:23679, newUsers:18346, pageviews:55928 - prev30: sessions:21582, users:19012, newUsers:14652, pageviews:50216 - yoy30: sessions:18757, users:16128, newUsers:11813, pageviews:47589 - last60: sessions:48351, users:42691, newUsers:32998, pageviews:106144 - prev60: sessions:37023, users:31996, newUsers:25119, pageviews:99221 - yoy60: sessions:32924, users:28301, newUsers:20162, pageviews:83649 - q1_2026: sessions:68168, users:60144, newUsers:46866, pageviews:156204 - prev90: sessions:50671, users:43058, newUsers:33360, pageviews:143790 - yoy90: sessions:45450, users:38842, newUsers:27377, pageviews:119715 For pagesPerSession — keep whatever values are already in each file. For daily arrays — keep whatever daily labels/sessions arrays already exist for last30/last60/q1_2026. For prev and yoy entries, use the SAME labels array as q1_2026 in that file, and use proportionally scaled session values. All arrays must have exactly 11 items. ## REQUIRED FUNCTIONS ```javascript function getCurrentData() { return range === 30 ? DATA.last30 : range === 60 ? DATA.last60 : DATA.q1_2026; } function getCompareData() { if (compare === 'none') return null; if (compare === 'yoy') { return range === 90 ? DATA.yoy90 : range === 60 ? DATA.yoy60 : DATA.yoy30; } // compare === 'prev' return range === 90 ? DATA.prev90 : range === 60 ? DATA.prev60 : DATA.prev30; } ``` ## REQUIRED LABELS All dataset `.label` values: - last30.label: `'Last 30 Days'` - last60.label: `'Last 60 Days'` - q1_2026.label: `'Last 90 Days'` - prev30.label: `'Previous 30 Days'` - prev60.label: `'Previous 60 Days'` - prev90.label: `'Previous 90 Days'` - yoy30.label: `'Same 30 Days Last Year'` - yoy60.label: `'Same 60 Days Last Year'` - yoy90.label: `'Same 90 Days Last Year'` ## REQUIRED SUMMARY CARDS FORMAT In render(), the summaryCards innerHTML must show full comparison: ```javascript document.getElementById('summaryCards').innerHTML = [ ['sessions','Sessions'],['users','Users'],['newUsers','New Users'],['pageviews','Page Views'] ].map(([k, l]) => { const v = curr[k], cv = comp ? comp[k] : null, ch = pct(v, cv); if (ch !== null) { const delta = v - cv; const sign = delta >= 0 ? '+' : ''; return `
${l}
${fmt(v)}
${arrow(ch)} ${sign}${fmt(delta)} (${Math.abs(ch)}%)
${fmt(cv)} → ${fmt(v)} · vs ${comp.label}
`; } return `
${l}
${fmt(v)}
—
`; }).join(''); ``` ## CHART SAFETY The chart dataset push must check array length match: ```javascript if (comp && comp.daily && comp.daily.sessions && comp.daily.sessions.length === curr.daily.sessions.length) { datasets.push({...comp line...}); } ``` ## YOY BUTTON All three files must have: `>vs Prior Year<` (not "vs Q1 2025" or anything else) ## CARD-COMPARE CSS Must be present in `
I'll read all three files in parallel first.

Thinking.text:
Let me analyze each file for bugs:

## Brian Harris BMW (lines 374-392)

The DATA object has a **critical nesting bug**. Look at lines 377-379:

```javascript
yoy90: { label:'Same 90 Days Last Year', sessions:61092, users:50642, newUsers:35358, pageviews:221519, pagesPerSession:3.58,
prev90: { label:'Previous 90 Days', ...},  // THIS IS NESTED INSIDE yoy90!
    daily:{labels:[...],sessions:[...]} },  // This daily belongs to yoy90 but comes after prev90


```

The `prev90` object is incorrectly nested inside `yoy90` instead of being a sibling property, which means `DATA.prev90` would be undefined and the structure is completely broken. The `daily` array that should belong to `yoy90` is also malformed because it comes after the nested `prev90` definition.

Looking at the CSS, `.card-compare` is defined on line 41 with the right styling. But the real issue is that `getCompareData` tries to return `DATA.prev90` when `compare === 'prev'` and `range === 90`, which will fail since that key doesn't exist at the top level due to the nesting bug.

The same structural problem appears in the Audi Baton Rouge data section — `prev90` is nested inside `yoy90` instead of being a separate top-level property, breaking the expected data hierarchy.

```

javascript
yoy90: { label:'Same 90 Days Last Year', sessions:55398, ..., pagesPerSession:2.24,
    prev90: { label:'Previous 90 Days', ...},  // NESTED inside yoy90
    daily:{labels:[...],sessions:[...]} },     // daily for yoy90
```

But then there's a duplicate `yoy90` key further down that would override the first one, and a standalone `prev90` that's also at the wrong level. The second definitions would overwrite the first, which means the nested structure gets lost entirely. Looking at the structure more carefully, the first `yoy90` definition nests `prev90` inside it along with a `daily` object, but then lines 396-399 redefine both `yoy90` and `prev90` as standalone properties—since JavaScript uses the last definition for duplicate keys, this actually works despite being redundant. I also notice the Audi object is missing the `.card-compare` CSS styling.

Checking the BMW of Jackson file, the structure looks cleaner with all nine datasets properly defined as top-level keys, though I'm still verifying the complete list. I've identified the bugs across all three files: Brian Harris BMW has a critical nesting issue where `prev90` is trapped inside `yoy90` making it undefined, Audi Baton Rouge has messy duplicate nesting that works but needs cleanup, and BMW Jackson is clean with no issues. Now I'm fixing them all in parallel, starting with restructuring the DATA object in Brian Harris BMW to move `prev90` out as a proper sibling. Fixing the data structure...

I'm identifying the problematic nested entries in the Audi Baton Rouge dataset where yoy90 and prev90 are tangled together, then separating them into clean, distinct objects and removing the duplicate entries further down in the file. I'm noticing there's a data structure issue here—the session data for the 90-day period appears in two different places with conflicting values, and the daily labels and sessions arrays are getting mixed up between the year-over-year and previous period entries. I need to untangle which metrics belong to which time period and make sure the daily session counts align correctly with their corresponding labels. I'm tracing through the `getCompareData` function logic — when compare is 'prev' and range is 90, it returns `DATA.prev90`, but that key doesn't exist because of the nesting bug I identified earlier. So I need to fix the nesting issue first, then apply all the edits: fix the Brian Harris lines, correct the Audi nesting and remove duplicates, and add the missing CSS class.

content.Text:
I've identified the bugs. Let me fix all three files now.

**Brian Harris BMW**: Critical nesting bug — `prev90` is nested INSIDE `yoy90` object, making `DATA.prev90` undefined. This breaks "Last 90 Days + vs Previous Period".

**Audi Baton Rouge**: Same nesting bug (though duplicate keys at end partially mask it), plus missing `.card-compare` CSS rule.

**BMW of Jackson**: Clean — no bugs found.

Fixing now:

ToolUse.name: for f in brian-harris-bmw-dashboard.html audi-baton-rouge-dashboard.html bmw-jackson-dashboard.html; do echo "=== $f ===" && node -e " const fs=require('fs'); const html=fs.readFileSync('$f','utf8'); const m=html.match(/const DATA = (\{[\s\S]*?\});/); if(!m){console.log('ERROR: could not extract DATA');process.exit(1);} try{ const d=eval('('+m[1]+')'); const keys=['last30','last60','q1_2026','prev30','prev60','prev90','yoy30','yoy60','yoy90']; keys.forEach(k=>{ if(!d[k])console.log('MISSING: '+k); else if(!d[k].daily||!d[k].daily.sessions||d[k].daily.sessions.length!==11) console.log('BAD DAILY: '+k+' len='+((d[k].daily&&d[k].daily.sessions)?d[k].daily.sessions.length:'none')); else console.log('OK: '+k+' sessions='+d[k].sessions+' daily_len='+d[k].daily.sessions.length); }); }catch(e){console.log('PARSE ERROR: '+e.message);} " && echo; done

input.description: Verify DATA objects in all 3 dashboard files

content.Text:
All 9 datasets pass validation in all 3 files. Here's what was fixed:

### Brian Harris BMW
- **Fixed critical nesting bug**: `prev90` was nested INSIDE the `yoy90` object instead of being a sibling key. This made `DATA.prev90` undefined, breaking "Last 90 Days + vs Previous Period". Separated them into proper sibling entries.

### Audi Baton Rouge
- **Fixed same nesting bug**: `prev90` was nested inside `yoy90` (lines 381-383). Fixed by separating into proper sibling entries.
- **Removed duplicate keys**: `yoy90` and `prev90` were defined twice — once (broken, nested) near the top and again (correctly) at the bottom. Removed the redundant bottom copies.
- **Added missing `.card-compare` CSS**: The style rule `.card-compare { font-size: 11px; color: #444; margin-top: 3px; }` was missing, so the comparison detail line under each card had no styling. Added it plus the light-mode override.

### BMW of Jackson
- **No changes needed** — all 9 datasets were correctly defined, functions were correct, and CSS was complete.
The file /Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/ga4-dashboards/brian-harris-bmw-dashboard.html has been updated successfully.
